title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Shapes Drill
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly three sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='22'/><rect x='75' y='18' width='44' height='44'/><polygon points='170,18 192,62 148,62'/><polygon points='220,20 260,20 270,60 210,60'/><polygon points='285,40 300,25 315,40 310,60 290,60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes in a row: circle, square, triangle, trapezoid, pentagon.
questions.solution: A triangle is the shape that has exactly three sides.

questions.question_text: Ella drew circles on a poster. How many circles did Ella draw in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='18'/><rect x='65' y='22' width='36' height='36'/><circle cx='135' cy='40' r='18'/><polygon points='175,22 195,58 155,58'/><circle cx='230' cy='40' r='18'/><circle cx='275' cy='40' r='18'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Row of shapes: circle, square, circle, triangle, circle, circle.
questions.solution: Counting only the circles gives 4 circles.

questions.question_text: Tom has four shape cards. Which card shows a square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='25' width='60' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><circle cx='170' cy='40' r='22'/><polygon points='215,15 245,55 195,55'/><polygon points='265,20 295,20 285,55 260,55'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: rectangle, square, circle, triangle, trapezoid.
questions.solution: A square has four equal sides and four right angles.

questions.question_text: Noah drew a design using small triangles. How many small triangles did Noah use in the design?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,60 45,20 70,60'/><polygon points='95,60 120,20 145,60'/><rect x='170' y='25' width='35' height='35'/><polygon points='230,60 255,20 280,60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three triangles and one square shown in a row.
questions.solution: There are 3 triangles and 1 square, so 3 triangles in total.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has how many corners?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='40' y='25' width='120' height='50'/><circle cx='40' cy='25' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='160' cy='25' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='40' cy='75' r='3' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='160' cy='75' r='3' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with its four corners marked as dots.
questions.solution: A rectangle has 4 corners.

questions.question_text: Which shape has no corners at all?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='75,20 105,60 55,60'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='22'/><rect x='190' y='25' width='60' height='30'/><polygon points='265,20 295,20 285,55 260,55'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: square, triangle, circle, rectangle, trapezoid.
questions.solution: A circle has no corners because it is curved all around.

questions.question_text: Count the squares in the picture.
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='18'/><rect x='120' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='190,20 215,60 165,60'/><rect x='235' y='20' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of shapes: square, circle, square, triangle, square.
questions.solution: Looking at the row, there are 3 squares.

questions.question_text: Which of these shapes is round and has no straight sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='15,60 40,20 65,60'/><rect x='85' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='145' y='28' width='60' height='24'/><circle cx='235' cy='40' r='20'/><polygon points='275,22 295,22 285,58 265,58'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: triangle, square, rectangle, circle, trapezoid.
questions.solution: A circle is round and has no straight sides.

questions.question_text: How many sides does a pentagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,15 105,48 88,100 32,100 15,48'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A regular pentagon.
questions.solution: A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.question_text: Maya arranged stickers shaped like triangles on her notebook. How many triangle stickers are on Maya's notebook?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 320 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='15,60 40,20 65,60'/><polygon points='80,60 105,20 130,60'/><rect x='145' y='25' width='30' height='30'/><polygon points='195,60 220,20 245,60'/><polygon points='255,60 280,20 305,60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four triangles and one square arranged in a row.
questions.solution: Counting only the triangles, there are 4 triangles.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly six sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,20 40,20 30,55'/><rect x='65' y='18' width='38' height='38'/><polygon points='145,18 165,18 175,36 165,54 145,54 135,36'/><polygon points='200,18 225,18 230,40 212,56 195,40'/><circle cx='270' cy='38' r='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: triangle, square, hexagon, pentagon, circle.
questions.solution: A hexagon is the shape with exactly 6 sides.

questions.question_text: Two identical right triangles are placed together along their long sides. What shape can they form?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,80 60,20 100,80'/><polygon points='120,20 200,20 160,80'/><line x1='120' y1='20' x2='160' y2='80' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two right triangles; the second picture shows them placed together along the long side.
questions.solution: Two right triangles joined along the long side can form a square.

questions.question_text: How many squares are in this figure made of 4 small squares placed in a 2 by 2 grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four small squares arranged in a 2 by 2 grid forming one large square.
questions.solution: There are 4 small squares and 1 large square made from all of them together, giving 5 squares in total.

questions.question_text: Which picture shows a shape that is the same on both sides of the dotted line?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><g><polygon points='20,80 45,20 70,80'/><line x1='45' y1='15' x2='45' y2='85' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></g><g><polygon points='95,20 135,20 125,80 85,80'/><line x1='110' y1='15' x2='110' y2='85' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></g><g><path d='M170 80 L180 40 L200 50 L210 30 L220 80 Z'/><line x1='195' y1='15' x2='195' y2='85' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three shapes with dotted vertical lines: a symmetric triangle, a slanted parallelogram, and an irregular shape.
questions.solution: Only the isosceles triangle is the same on both sides of the line, so it has line symmetry.

questions.question_text: Maya draws a shape with 4 equal sides and 4 equal corners. What shape did she draw?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='30' y='30' width='60' height='60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with all sides equal.
questions.solution: A shape with 4 equal sides and 4 equal corners is a square.

questions.question_text: How many corners does the star shape in the picture have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 70,45 105,45 77,65 87,100 60,78 33,100 43,65 15,45 50,45'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A five-pointed star.
questions.solution: A five-pointed star has 5 outer points and 5 inner corners, giving 10 corners in total.

questions.question_text: The picture is made of circles, triangles, and squares. How many shapes are there all together?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 320 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='25' cy='40' r='16'/><polygon points='55,60 75,25 95,60'/><rect x='105' y='22' width='32' height='32'/><circle cx='160' cy='40' r='16'/><polygon points='185,60 205,25 225,60'/><rect x='235' y='22' width='32' height='32'/><circle cx='290' cy='40' r='16'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Row of shapes: 3 circles, 2 triangles, 2 squares.
questions.solution: There are 3 circles, 2 triangles, and 2 squares, so 3 + 2 + 2 = 7 shapes.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is cut into two equal pieces by a single straight line. Which new shape is NOT possible?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 320 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='30' width='80' height='40'/><line x1='60' y1='30' x2='60' y2='70' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><rect x='120' y='30' width='80' height='40'/><line x1='120' y1='30' x2='200' y2='70' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><rect x='220' y='30' width='80' height='40'/><line x1='220' y1='50' x2='300' y2='50' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three rectangles with different cuts: a vertical cut, a diagonal cut, and a horizontal cut.
questions.solution: A straight cut can make rectangles, squares, or triangles, but not circles because circles have no straight sides.

questions.question_text: Count the total number of straight sides in all the shapes shown.
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='15,60 40,20 65,60'/><rect x='85' y='22' width='38' height='38'/><polygon points='150,18 170,18 180,38 170,58 150,58 140,38'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle, a square, and a hexagon shown in a row.
questions.solution: Triangle has 3 sides, square has 4, hexagon has 6. 3 + 4 + 6 = 13.

questions.question_text: The pattern repeats: circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle, ... What is the 8th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 320 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='45' y='18' width='24' height='24'/><polygon points='85,42 100,15 115,42'/><circle cx='135' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='160' y='18' width='24' height='24'/><polygon points='200,42 215,15 230,42'/><circle cx='250' cy='30' r='12'/><text x='280' y='34' fill='currentColor' font-size='16'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of circle, square, triangle; the 8th position is marked with a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes. Positions 7, 8, 9 are circle, square, triangle. So the 8th shape is a square.

questions.question_text: A square is cut by a straight line going from one corner to the opposite corner. What two shapes are made?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='80'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='100' y2='100'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with a diagonal line drawn from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner.
questions.solution: A diagonal cut across a square makes two triangles.

questions.question_text: In the picture, how many shapes have exactly 4 corners?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 340 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='35' height='35'/><polygon points='60,55 80,20 100,55'/><rect x='115' y='28' width='50' height='22'/><circle cx='190' cy='38' r='16'/><polygon points='220,20 250,20 260,38 250,55 220,55 210,38'/><rect x='280' y='20' width='35' height='35'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Six shapes in a row: square, triangle, rectangle, circle, hexagon, square.
questions.solution: The shapes with 4 corners are the two squares and the rectangle, for a total of 3 shapes.

questions.question_text: Leo folds a square piece of paper exactly in half along a straight line. Which shape can he NOT get?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='15' y='20' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='15' y1='50' x2='75' y2='50' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><rect x='95' y='20' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='125' y1='20' x2='125' y2='80' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><rect x='175' y='20' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='175' y1='20' x2='235' y2='80' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three squares with different fold lines: horizontal, vertical, and diagonal.
questions.solution: Folding a square along a straight line gives two rectangles or two triangles, but never a hexagon.

questions.question_text: A big triangle is split into equal smaller triangles as shown, with one small triangle on top and a row of three on the bottom. How many small triangles are in the big triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 140' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='80,15 145,125 15,125'/><polygon points='80,15 112,70 48,70'/><line x1='48' y1='70' x2='112' y2='70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided into 4 smaller equal triangles.
questions.solution: The big triangle is made of 4 smaller equal triangles.
